reasoning: The 2028 election will be an open race since Donald Trump (R) won in 2024 and is constitutionally term-limited from seeking a third term. Historically, open elections have favored the out-party (in this case, the Democrats). Prediction markets and betting odds as of March and April 2026 generally place the Democrats' chances of winning the White House at roughly 57% to 60%. The Metaculus community prediction stands slightly higher at 0.63. Given the historical base rate advantage for the out-party in term-limited open elections, combined with recent market data, a probability of 60% reflects both the structural advantage and the significant inherent uncertainty remaining 2.5 years before the election.
